title: Multi-Head Self-Attention
description:
Implement multi-head scaled dot-product self-attention for queries, keys, and values $Q, K, V \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times d}$ (sequence length $n$, model dim $d$) split into $h$ heads of size $d_h = d/h$.

For head $k = 0, \dots, h-1$, let $Q_k, K_k, V_k \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times d_h}$ be the columns $[k \cdot d_h : (k+1) \cdot d_h]$ of $Q, K, V$. Compute
$$\text{head}_k = \text{softmax}\!\left(\frac{Q_k K_k^\top}{\sqrt{d_h}}\right)V_k$$
using a numerically stable row-wise softmax (subtract the row max first), then concatenate the heads along the last axis:
$$\text{MultiHead}(Q,K,V) = [\text{head}_0, \dots, \text{head}_{h-1}] \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times d}$$

Implement `multi_head_attention(Q, K, V, num_heads)` returning the output of shape `(n, d)`.

**Shapes**: `Q`, `K`, `V`: `(4, 8)` (seq_len=4, d=8), `num_heads`: `2` (constant; d/num_heads=4) -> returns `(4, 8)`.

reference:
import numpy as np

def multi_head_attention(Q, K, V, num_heads):
    Q = np.asarray(Q, dtype=np.float64)
    K = np.asarray(K, dtype=np.float64)
    V = np.asarray(V, dtype=np.float64)
    d = Q.shape[-1]
    h = num_heads
    dh = d // h
    outputs = []
    for k in range(h):
        Qk = Q[:, k*dh:(k+1)*dh]
        Kk = K[:, k*dh:(k+1)*dh]
        Vk = V[:, k*dh:(k+1)*dh]
        scores = (Qk @ Kk.T) / np.sqrt(dh)
        scores = scores - scores.max(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
        e = np.exp(scores)
        weights = e / e.sum(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
        outputs.append(weights @ Vk)
    return np.concatenate(outputs, axis=-1)
hints: Slice the last dimension (size d) of Q, K, V into num_heads contiguous chunks of size d/num_heads, one per head.

Run the standard scaled dot-product attention (softmax(Qk @ Kk.T / sqrt(head_dim)) @ Vk) independently on each head's chunks, then np.concatenate the per-head outputs along the last axis in head order.

solutions.name: Split into heads, attend independently, concatenate
solutions.explanation: Multi-head attention runs $h$ independent scaled dot-product attentions, each on a $d/h$-sized slice of $Q,K,V$, so every head can learn to attend over a different subspace. The per-head outputs (each $n \times d_h$) are concatenated back along the feature axis to restore the original width $d$.
solutions.code:
import numpy as np
def multi_head_attention(Q, K, V, num_heads):
    Q = np.asarray(Q, dtype=np.float64)
    K = np.asarray(K, dtype=np.float64)
    V = np.asarray(V, dtype=np.float64)
    d = Q.shape[-1]
    h = num_heads
    dh = d // h
    outputs = []
    for k in range(h):
        Qk = Q[:, k*dh:(k+1)*dh]
        Kk = K[:, k*dh:(k+1)*dh]
        Vk = V[:, k*dh:(k+1)*dh]
        scores = (Qk @ Kk.T) / np.sqrt(dh)
        scores = scores - scores.max(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
        e = np.exp(scores)
        weights = e / e.sum(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
        outputs.append(weights @ Vk)
    return np.concatenate(outputs, axis=-1)
solutions.complexity: Time O(h * n^2 * d_h) = O(n^2 * d), Space O(n^2)
